_README: Material sourcing model: factory-direct (imported) vs US domestic wholesale, for outdoor fence & gate materials and other high-container-volume building materials. PROVENANCE is explicit on every price: 'sub' = a number Ori got directly from a subcontractor/supplier; 'online' = web research (Alibaba/Made-in-China listings, US distributor listings, tariff filings). See material-sourcing-README.md. Prices are MATERIAL ONLY (not installed). A 'box' = one pre-assembled fence PANEL section, NOT a bulk carton of loose footage.
unitNote: Fence 'box' = one pre-assembled panel. Vinyl & wood panels are typically 8 ft wide (~8 LF/box); aluminum panels ~6 ft wide (~6 LF/box). Always confirm LF-per-box with the supplier before comparing quotes — some factories price per panel/set, some per linear foot, with no consistent labeling.
dutyWarning: The Excel model Ori built compared FOB 'box cost' straight to a domestic price and skipped import duties + freight entirely. Real landed cost = FOB + duty + ocean freight + drayage + broker. Duties are large and moving in 2026 (see per-material dutyStack).

sub.note: Sub's price per 8-ft panel; sub imports factory-direct so this is already landed/pass-through. Excel used midpoint 47.5.

domesticWholesale.examples: Boulder economy $9.17/LF; Newbury premium $19.99/LF (a 4-ft economy panel dips to ~$5.75/LF but that isn't a 6-ft privacy comparison)

importSavingPct.note: Economy grade may be a WASH after ~40% duty + freight; savings are real only on mid/premium grades and at volume.

fenceGate.dutyStack: ~40.3% China origin (5.3% MFN + 25% Sec.301 + 10% Sec.122). Sec.122 10% expires ~2026-07-24 unless renewed — live regulatory change, confirm before ordering. HTS 3925.90.
fenceGate.importBreakEven: ~7,000-12,000+ LF/yr (2-3+ containers); below that, freight/broker/QC fixed costs eat the FOB discount.

gateKit.factoryDirect: not found — needs direct supplier RFQ

fenceGate.verdict: Buy vinyl THROUGH the sub — his ~$5-7/LF beats domestic wholesale $9-20/LF. Joon importing directly only pencils at 2-3+ containers/yr, and even then only on mid/premium grades.

fenceGate.portStatus_perSub: OUT OF STOCK (at port) — 'waiting for containers to be released'

sub.AMBIGUITY_FLAG: This is the ONE number to confirm with the sub. '$65/box' could be (a) a full 6-ft panel+post set = ~$10.83/LF (beats domestic — great deal), OR (b) a 3-4 LF picket bundle with no hardware = ~$16-18/LF (near domestic). Ask the sub: 'Is the $65 a full panel+post section, or a box of pickets?' The per-LF math differs ~2x. Excel divided by 8; a composite panel is 6 ft wide, so ÷6 is correct if it's a panel.

factoryDirectFOB.grades: standard co-extrusion $70-90; aluminum-post $90-120; premium fluted/dual-tone $100-140 (per 6-ft set)
factoryDirectFOB.note: Alibaba headline $2.60-5/m prices are 'bait' — real FOB is the set prices above. mecofence wholesale guide.

landedEstimate.freightNote: Ocean freight at JULY 2026 PEAK: $6,075-7,425 per 40ft (vs $2,100-2,700 in Jan). Peak Aug-Oct. This thins the import case right now.

importSavingPct.note: Vendor marketing claims 25-45%; real landed math at current ~40% duty + PEAK freight is closer to 0-30%. Clear win only at full-container (300+ LF) volume outside peak season.

fenceGate.dutyStack: ~40.3% China origin (5.3% MFN + 25% Sec.301 + 10% Sec.122). HTS 3925.90 (CBP ruling N337378 — plastic component governs). Same stack as vinyl.

gateKit.factoryDirect: not found — needs direct supplier RFQ

fenceGate.verdict: Depends entirely on what the '$65/box' is. If a full 6-ft panel: strong buy through the sub (~$11/LF vs $20-80 domestic). If a picket bundle: only modest edge. Confirm with the sub first. Direct import only pencils at full-container volume, not during the current peak-freight window.

fenceGate.portStatus_perSub: OUT OF STOCK (at port) — 'waiting for containers to be released'

sub.note: Sub's price per ~6-ft powder-coated panel. Excel divided by 8 (=$9.38/LF) — WRONG for aluminum; aluminum panels are ~6 ft wide, so per-LF is ~$12.50, not $9.38.

factoryDirectFOB.note: Alibaba/MIC quote per m2 or per piece, rarely a bundled post-to-post panel — hard to standardize; get a landed quote.

domesticWholesale.examples: Home Depot Forgeright $11.84/LF; FenceWorkshop 6-ft panels $80-245 ($13.40-40.84/LF); Barrette $28-29/LF

importSavingPct.note: This is the key finding — see dutyStack.

fenceGate.dutyStack: Chinese aluminum fence is inside the Antidumping/Countervailing Duty order on aluminum extrusions: AD China-wide 86.01% + CVD 7.37% (up to 374%) + Section 232 aluminum 50% (as of 2025-06-04) + Section 301 ~25%. Stacking AD (86%) + Sec.232 (50%) alone more than DOUBLES landed cost over FOB. HTS aluminum extrusions scope ruling (trade.gov ita-338p).
fenceGate.verdict: DO NOT import aluminum fence direct from China — tariffs erase the FOB discount several times over. The sub's $75/panel is ~= domestic ($71-100 for a comparable 6-ft panel), which corroborates this: even 'factory-direct' aluminum lands near domestic once duties are paid. Buy aluminum domestically (or from the sub at domestic-parity), whichever is more reliable.

sub.note: Sub's price per 8-ft PT pine panel. Matches Home Depot 6ft x 8ft PT pine panel ($49.98-54.98) — i.e. this is a DOMESTIC panel, not an import.

factoryDirectFOB.value: N/A — no viable China factory-direct supply chain for solid-sawn PT pine/cedar/redwood fence panels

sources: Home Depot PT pine fence panels

importSavingPct.value: N/A — importing wood does not make sense
importSavingPct.note: Low value-to-weight (freight dominates), abundant nearby domestic supply (SE-US pine, PNW/NorCal cedar-redwood), no real Chinese solid-lumber fence export industry. CONFIRMED.

fenceGate.verdict: Wood is a domestic-only category. The Excel treating wood as an 'overseas' line is misleading — the sub's $55 is a domestic pre-assembled panel. Source locally.

otherMaterials.dutyNote: No China AD/CVD on vinyl flooring (HTS 3918); general China stack 5.3% MFN + 25% Sec.301 + 10% Sec.122. Real ENGINEERED HARDWOOD is different — separate Multilayered Wood Flooring AD/CVD, stay domestic.
otherMaterials.containerCoverage: 40HQ ~7,000-13,000 sqft = under 1 yr of GC use
otherMaterials.verdict: IMPORT NOW — strongest direct-import case. Vinyl-core only, not engineered hardwood.

otherMaterials.dutyNote: China quartz AD 265-337% + CVD 45-191% (~300-430% combined) — kills China import. Vietnam currently exempt from that order (~20% general) but Section 201 global safeguard up to 50% cleared ITC injury vote 2026-04-01, decision mid-2026.
otherMaterials.verdict: China: stay domestic. Vietnam: import at volume, but needs a fabricator relationship + warehouse; Section 201 risk makes a single-container bet speculative now.

otherMaterials.dutyNote: China ceramic tile AD/CVD continued Feb 2026, commonly cited 600-725% China-wide (verify vs primary order). India also now AD/CVD. Country-by-country reciprocal rates in flux post-SCOTUS Feb 2026.
otherMaterials.verdict: China: stay domestic (punitive duty). Verified non-China/non-AD-CVD origin: import at volume — widest raw FOB spread of the set, survives a moderate general tariff. Needs storage + breakage tolerance.
sources: Federal Register ceramic tile AD/CVD continuation 2026

otherMaterials.dutyNote: China Wooden Cabinets AD/CVD up to 251.64% (2024-25 review) + Vietnam circumvention ruling (Chinese components) + Section 232 25% on cabinets from ALL countries (2025-10-14, rising to 50% Jan 2027).
otherMaterials.verdict: STAY DOMESTIC — full stop. Clearest 'don't' in the list. No low-duty sourcing country left at GC scale.

otherMaterials.dutyNote: ~22.5% (Sec.301 + Sec.122); no China AD/CVD on finished vinyl windows. The REAL gate is CA Title 24 / NFRC certification — every permitted window needs an NFRC U-factor/SHGC label; only a handful of Asian factories carry NFRC listing (multi-month, multi-$K per product line).
otherMaterials.verdict: Stay domestic for spot/project buys. Import only at 50+ units/yr committed to ONE NFRC-certified factory line — below that, compliance friction + narrowed choice eat the savings. Matches the earlier 'windows are dealer-only' read.
